generated_summary: Re-quotes a URI string by first unquoting any percent-encoded unreserved characters and then re-encoding them, preserving characters that were already correctly encoded.
Parses the Content-Type header of an HTTP response and extracts the charset encoding using the cgi module, returning None if no encoding is specified.
Selects and returns the appropriate proxy URL from a dictionary of proxies based on the URL scheme of the request.
Merges request-level and session-level settings into a single dictionary, though it does not always clearly prioritize request-level values over session defaults.
Constructs the full URL for a prepared HTTP request by combining the scheme, host, path, and query components.
Recursively flattens an arbitrarily nested iterable, yielding individual non-iterable elements one at a time.
Checks whether an object is list-like by testing for iterability and length, with an optional allow_sets parameter to exclude set types.
Returns the unique values from an array-like input in the order they first appear, using a hash table internally for efficiency.
Concatenates pandas DataFrame or Series objects along a specified axis, combining their data into a single object.
Converts scalar, array-like, Series, or DataFrame input to pandas Timestamp or DatetimeIndex objects, with configurable format inference and error-handling modes.
Validates an input array by checking and optionally enforcing dtype, dimensionality, and sparsity constraints, converting it to the desired format.
Jointly validates feature matrix X and target array y for compatible shapes and data types, delegating to check_array for each.
Splits arrays or matrices into random train and test subsets, supporting stratified splitting and reproducibility via a random state parameter.
Computes the cosine similarity between pairs of samples in the input matrices, with support for both dense and sparse matrix inputs.
Scales each sample individually to unit norm using L2 normalization, incorrectly modifying the input array in-place by default.
